medical_history.chief_complaint: Hyperkalemia (7.0 mEq/l) that had been incidentally identified when she was treated for a cold
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: In August 2002, hyperkalemia (7.0 mEq/l) was incidentally identified. In March 2003, she was again diagnosed as having hyperkalemia (6.0 mEq/l). She was admitted to the hospital for evaluation of the cause of hyperkalemia on May 8, 2003. She did not have any history of disease and did not take any medications.
medical_history.family_history: Her father also had elevated plasma potassium levels at 4°C and 25°C, particularly at 4°C.

physical_examination.general_examination: Temperature was 36.0°C, pulse rate was 70 beats per minute, respiratory rate was 14 breaths per minute, and blood pressure was 111/60 mmHg. The chest was clear on auscultation, heart sounds were regular, and no murmur was heard. Abdominal examination revealed no abnormalities. No edema of the extremities was found.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: No neurological abnormalities, such as paresthesia or muscle weakness, were identified.
